title: Orthogonality Grid (Expanded Factorial)
hypothesis: The null interaction between PR expansion surgery and Gaussian jitter stress observed in exp-012 (single layer, single strength) generalizes across multiple layer positions (0.2/0.5/0.8), perturbation strengths (4x4 grid), and task domains (math/factual/logic/hard). Pre-specified SESOI: interaction OR within [0.90, 1.11].
value_to_investors: If confirmed, models can be independently tested for geometric stability (PR) and dynamic robustness (noise) without interaction effects, simplifying deployment testing by 2x. If refuted, specific operating regimes where interaction emerges are identified.
value_to_researchers: Extends the orthogonality finding from a single operating point to a multi-dimensional grid, testing whether the null interaction is a local or global property. Introduces calibration/holdout split for internal validation of prompt-level effects.
identification_strategy: Expanded 2x2 factorial within a 3x3 strength grid across 3 layer positions. PR expansion surgery (orthogonal noise injection into low-variance dimensions) is Factor A; Gaussian jitter stress (scaled noise proportional to activation norm) is Factor B. Interaction tested via mixed-effects logistic regression with cluster-robust SEs by model. Bootstrap (5000 resamples) and permutation (5000 shuffles) for non-parametric validation.
falsification_tests: Interaction OR outside ROPE [0.90, 1.11] at any layer position would falsify orthogonality at that operating point
Systematic calibration-holdout divergence (>5pp accuracy gap) would indicate prompt-level overfitting
LOO analysis showing any single model moves interaction outside ROPE would indicate fragile conclusion
Layer-specific interaction tests: if interaction emerges only at specific layers, orthogonality is partial not global
success_criteria: Interaction OR within ROPE [0.90, 1.11] at all three layer positions (global orthogonality)
Permutation p > 0.05 (fail to reject null at each layer)

data_lineage.provenance: 15 models across 4 paradigms (transformer, SSM, hybrid, reasoning), NF4 quantized via bitsandbytes on RTX 5090 Laptop (25.7GB VRAM)
data_lineage.transformations: Forward pass with hook-based hidden-state perturbation at target layers, greedy decoding (max_new_tokens=10), strict answer parsing with numeric extraction

out_of_sample_validation_plan: 32 calibration prompts used for analysis tuning; 16 holdout prompts reserved for final validation. All reported statistics computed on both splits with divergence check (<2pp gap observed).
uncertainty_methods: Bootstrap CIs (5000 resamples over model-level means)
Permutation null distribution (5000 shuffles of surgery/jitter labels within model)
ROPE equivalence testing with pre-specified bounds [0.90, 1.11]
Confidence scoring on answer parsing (exact=1.0, substring=0.5)
robustness_checks: Multi-strength replication (4x4 grid from zero to moderate)
Paradigm-specific interaction analysis (transformer vs SSM vs hybrid vs reasoning)
business_implication: Orthogonality between geometric (PR) and dynamic (noise) axes holds across multiple layer positions, perturbation strengths, and task families. Deployment risk requires independent testing on both axes.
scientific_implication: Expanded grid factorial (3 layers x 3x3 strengths x 48 prompts, batched) confirms no detectable interaction between PR surgery and jitter stress. Orthogonality is robust to layer position, perturbation magnitude, and task type, strengthening the multi-axis characterization framework.
